evals.prompt: Deploy my Power Pages site. I'm already authenticated and the environment is correct. The project is in the current directory.
evals.expected_output: The skill verifies PAC CLI installation, confirms authentication via pac auth who, presents the current environment for user confirmation, locates powerpages.config.json, runs npm run build, executes pac pages upload-code-site, verifies the .powerpages-site folder exists, commits deployment artifacts, checks activation status, and offers to activate or suggests next steps.
expectations: PAC CLI is verified by running pac help
Authentication is checked via pac auth who
Current environment name and URL are presented to the user for confirmation
Project root is located by finding powerpages.config.json
npm run build is executed before uploading
pac pages upload-code-site is run with the correct --rootPath
.powerpages-site folder is verified to exist after upload
Deployment artifacts are committed via git add -A and git commit
Activation status is checked using the check-activation-status.js script
If site is not activated, user is asked whether to activate now or skip
Next steps are suggested including activate-site, test-site, setup-datamodel, and add-seo
TaskCreate is used to create a task list covering all 6 phases before starting work

evals.prompt: I want to push my code site to Power Pages. I haven't set up PAC CLI auth yet.
evals.expected_output: The skill verifies PAC CLI is installed, detects that the user is not authenticated via pac auth who, asks the user for their environment URL, runs pac auth create with the provided URL, verifies authentication succeeded, then proceeds with environment confirmation, build, upload, verification, and post-deployment steps.
expectations: PAC CLI is verified by running pac help
pac auth who is run and the unauthenticated status is detected
User is informed they are not authenticated with PAC CLI
AskUserQuestion is used to request the environment URL from the user
pac auth create --environment is run with the user-provided URL
pac auth who is run again to verify authentication succeeded
After successful auth, the environment is presented for confirmation
Project root is located, site is built with npm run build, and uploaded with pac pages upload-code-site
Post-deployment verification is performed including .powerpages-site check and git commit
TaskCreate is used upfront to track all phases

evals.prompt: Deploy my site to Power Pages. I've been getting errors about JavaScript files being blocked.
evals.expected_output: The skill completes pre-deployment checks, attempts the upload which fails due to blocked .js attachments, explains the issue to the user, asks for permission to unblock .js, retrieves the current blocked attachments list via pac env list-settings, removes js from the list, updates the setting via pac env update-settings, retries the upload successfully, and proceeds to verify deployment.
expectations: PAC CLI and authentication are verified before attempting upload
Environment is confirmed with the user
pac pages upload-code-site is attempted and the blocked JavaScript error is detected
User is informed that .js attachments are blocked in the environment
AskUserQuestion is used to ask permission before modifying the blocked attachments setting
pac env list-settings is run to retrieve the current blockedattachments value
The js extension is removed from the semicolon-separated blocked attachments list
pac env update-settings is run with the updated list (without js)
pac pages upload-code-site is retried after unblocking
Post-deployment verification proceeds normally after successful retry
Git commit is made with deployment artifacts

evals.prompt: Upload my code site to Power Pages. I previously deployed to a different environment and now I'm getting an error about .html attachments being blocked.
evals.expected_output: The skill completes pre-deployment checks, attempts the upload which fails with a misleading HTML blocked attachment error, recognizes this is caused by a stale environment manifest in .powerpages-site, locates and deletes the old environment-specific manifest.yml file, retries the upload successfully, and proceeds to verify deployment.
expectations: PAC CLI and authentication are verified
Environment is confirmed with the user
pac pages upload-code-site is attempted and the HTML blocked attachment error is detected
The error is recognized as misleading — not an actual .html block issue
The .powerpages-site folder is inspected for stale environment manifest files matching *-manifest.yml
The old environment-specific manifest.yml file is deleted
The skill does NOT attempt to unblock .html in the environment's blocked attachments list
pac pages upload-code-site is retried after deleting the manifest
Post-deployment verification proceeds after successful retry including .powerpages-site check and git commit
Activation status is checked and next steps are suggested
